insurance_policy_documents.simple_description: Protects you financially if you're in a car accident, your vehicle is damaged, or someone sues you after a crash.
ideal_for: Anyone who owns or regularly drives a car
New drivers added to a family policy
People leasing or financing a vehicle (required by lenders)

liability_coverage.description: Pays for injuries or damage you cause to others in an accident
includes: Repair or replacement of other vehicles/property
Legal defense if you're sued after an accident

collision_coverage.description: Pays to repair or replace your car after a crash, regardless of fault
includes: Single-vehicle crashes (hitting a tree, pole, etc.)

comprehensive_coverage.description: Covers damage to your car from non-collision events

additional_protections.uninsured_motorist: Covers you if hit by a driver with no insurance
additional_protections.medical_payments: Helps pay medical bills for you/passengers regardless of fault
additional_protections.rental_reimbursement: Pays for a rental car while yours is being repaired
additional_protections.roadside_assistance: Towing, jump-starts, lockout service, tire changes

what_is_not_covered: Regular maintenance (oil changes, tire rotations, brake pads)
Wear and tear (bald tires, worn belts, rust)
Using your car for ride-sharing without proper endorsement
Intentional damage or illegal activities
Driving under the influence (claims may be denied)
Personal items stolen from your vehicle (covered under homeowners/renters)
Mechanical breakdowns (requires separate mechanical breakdown policy)
when_to_recommend: User asks: 'I just bought a car, what insurance do I need?'
User asks: 'Am I covered if someone hits me and has no insurance?'
User asks: 'Does my insurance cover a rental car?'
User had an accident and needs to understand their coverage
insurance_policy_documents.simple_recommendation_script: If you drive a car, auto insurance is legally required in most states. This policy protects you from huge costs if you cause an accident, and covers repairs to your own vehicle too. We recommend liability + collision + comprehensive for full protection.
insurance_policy_documents.estimated_premium_range: $80-$250/month (varies by age, driving record, location, vehicle)
insurance_policy_documents.key_exclusions_summary: Does not cover maintenance, intentional acts, or using car for commercial ride-share without proper coverage.

insurance_policy_documents.simple_description: Protects your home, belongings, and finances if your house is damaged, stolen from, or if someone gets hurt on your property.
ideal_for: Homeowners (single-family, condo, townhouse)
People with mortgages (required by lenders)
Families wanting to protect personal belongings

dwelling_coverage.description: Repairs or rebuilds your home's structure after covered damage

personal_property.description: Replaces your belongings if damaged or stolen
includes: Items stolen from your car or while traveling
personal_property.optional_upgrade: Replacement cost endorsement (vs actual cash value)

liability_protection.description: Covers legal/medical costs if someone is injured on your property
includes: Accidental damage you cause to others' property

additional_living_expenses.description: Pays for temporary housing if your home is uninhabitable

what_is_not_covered: Flood damage (requires separate flood insurance)
Earthquake damage (requires endorsement or separate policy)
Normal wear and tear, maintenance issues (roof leaks from age)
Mold, pest infestations, or gradual water damage
Business equipment or home-based business liability
High-value items (jewelry, art) beyond sub-limits without scheduling
Intentional damage or neglect
when_to_recommend: User asks: 'I'm buying a house, what insurance do I need?'
User asks: 'If a tree falls on my house, am I covered?'
User asks: 'Does my insurance cover my laptop if it's stolen?'
User had a guest injured at their home and is worried about liability
insurance_policy_documents.simple_recommendation_script: Homeowners insurance is essential if you own a home. It protects your biggest investment—your house—and your stuff inside. It also shields you financially if someone gets hurt on your property. We recommend dwelling + personal property + liability + loss of use as a baseline.
insurance_policy_documents.estimated_premium_range: $100-$300/month (varies by home value, location, construction, claims history)
insurance_policy_documents.key_exclusions_summary: Does not cover floods, earthquakes, wear-and-tear, or business use without endorsements.

insurance_policy_documents.simple_description: Affordable protection for your belongings and liability when you rent an apartment, condo, or house.
ideal_for: Apartment or house renters
College students living off-campus
Anyone who wants to protect their personal belongings

personal_property.description: Replaces your belongings if damaged or stolen
includes: Items stolen from your car or while traveling
Coverage for fire, theft, vandalism, water damage (from internal sources)

liability_protection.description: Covers costs if you accidentally injure someone or damage their property

additional_living_expenses.description: Helps pay for temporary housing if your rental becomes uninhabitable

optional_add_ons: Replacement cost for belongings (vs actual cash value)
Scheduled items for jewelry, electronics, musical instruments

what_is_not_covered: The physical building/structure (landlord's responsibility)
Flood or earthquake damage (requires separate policies)
Roommate's belongings (they need their own policy)
Business equipment or home-based business liability
High-value items beyond sub-limits without scheduling
Pests, mold, or maintenance issues
when_to_recommend: User asks: 'I rent an apartment, do I need insurance?'
User asks: 'If my apartment floods, are my things covered?'
User asks: 'Am I liable if a guest trips in my rental?'
User wants affordable protection for their belongings
insurance_policy_documents.simple_recommendation_script: Even though you don't own the building, your stuff inside is worth protecting. Renters insurance is cheap (often under $20/month) and covers your belongings, liability, and temporary housing if disaster strikes. Highly recommended for all renters.
insurance_policy_documents.key_exclusions_summary: Does not cover the building itself, floods/earthquakes, or roommate's items.

insurance_policy_documents.simple_description: Provides a tax-free payment to your loved ones if you pass away during the policy term. Simple, affordable protection for your family's financial future.
ideal_for: Parents with young children
Spouses who depend on your income
People with mortgages or debts they want covered
Anyone seeking affordable, straightforward life insurance

death_benefit.description: Tax-free lump sum paid to your beneficiaries if you die while policy is active
includes: Funds can be used for any purpose: mortgage, education, daily living
Option for level or decreasing death benefit

term_options.description: Choose coverage length that matches your needs
includes: Level premiums (price stays same throughout term)
Option to convert to permanent insurance later (on some policies)

living_benefits_riders.description: Access benefits early if facing serious illness
includes: Accelerated death benefit for terminal illness

what_is_not_covered: Death by suicide within first 2 years (standard contestability period)
Death while committing a felony or illegal activity
Death from high-risk activities not disclosed during underwriting
No cash value accumulation (unlike whole life insurance)
Coverage ends at term expiration (no payout if you outlive the term)
Pre-existing conditions may affect eligibility or pricing
when_to_recommend: User asks: 'How can I make sure my family is okay financially if I die?'
User asks: 'What's the most affordable way to get life insurance?'
User has a new baby or just bought a home
User wants to cover a mortgage or college costs
insurance_policy_documents.simple_recommendation_script: Term life insurance is the most affordable way to protect your loved ones. If something happens to you during the term, your family gets a tax-free payment to cover expenses. We recommend coverage equal to 10x your income or enough to cover debts + 5-10 years of living expenses.
insurance_policy_documents.estimated_premium_range: $20-$100/month for $500k policy (varies by age, health, term length)
insurance_policy_documents.key_exclusions_summary: Does not build cash value, coverage ends after term, suicide clause in first 2 years.

insurance_policy_documents.simple_description: Helps cover out-of-pocket medical costs your main health insurance doesn't pay—like deductibles, copays, and unexpected expenses.
ideal_for: People with high-deductible health plans
Families wanting extra protection for accidents or illnesses
Anyone worried about unexpected medical bills

hospital_indemnity.description: Cash benefit paid directly to you for hospital stays
includes: Fixed amount per day or per admission
Money can be used for any expense: bills, travel, childcare

critical_illness_coverage.description: Lump-sum payment if diagnosed with a serious illness
includes: Cancer, heart attack, stroke, kidney failure
Funds for treatment, travel, lost wages, home care

accident_coverage.description: Helps pay costs from accidental injuries

what_is_not_covered: Does NOT replace major medical/ACA health insurance
Pre-existing conditions may have waiting periods
Routine care not tied to a covered event (unless wellness rider)
Cosmetic procedures, experimental treatments
Injuries from illegal activities or extreme sports (unless endorsed)
Mental health/substance abuse treatment (unless specified)
when_to_recommend: User asks: 'My health insurance has a $5,000 deductible—how do I cover that?'
User asks: 'What if I get cancer or have a heart attack?'
User has an accident and is worried about ER costs
User wants extra peace of mind beyond their main health plan
insurance_policy_documents.simple_recommendation_script: Supplemental health insurance fills the gaps in your main health plan. If you get sick or hurt, it pays you cash to help cover deductibles, travel, or lost wages. It's affordable and works alongside your existing insurance—not instead of it.
insurance_policy_documents.key_exclusions_summary: Not a replacement for major medical insurance; pre-existing conditions may have limitations.

insurance_policy_documents.simple_description: Extra liability protection that kicks in when your auto or homeowners insurance limits are exhausted. Shields your assets and future income from large lawsuits.
ideal_for: Homeowners or car owners with significant assets
People with teenage drivers
Landlords, coaches, volunteers, or anyone with higher liability exposure
Anyone who wants to protect future wages from lawsuits

excess_liability.description: Pays after your underlying auto/home policy limits are used up
includes: Bodily injury lawsuits (e.g., serious car accident you caused)
Property damage claims (e.g., you damage expensive property)
Personal injury claims (libel, slander, false arrest)
Legal defense costs, even for groundless suits

broad_coverage_scope.description: Covers liability situations other policies might miss
includes: Liability as a landlord or volunteer
Coverage for household members and dependents

worldwide_protection.description: Coverage follows you anywhere in the world

what_is_not_covered: Your own injuries or property damage (this is liability-only)
Business or professional liability (requires separate policy)
Intentional acts, criminal activity, or contractual liabilities
Damage from floods, earthquakes, or wear-and-tear
Liability from owned aircraft, large watercraft, or exotic vehicles
Workers' compensation claims for household employees
when_to_recommend: User asks: 'What if I cause a serious accident and get sued for more than my auto insurance covers?'
User asks: 'I have savings/investments—how do I protect them from lawsuits?'
User has teen drivers, a pool, dog, or rental property
User wants affordable extra protection beyond standard policies
insurance_policy_documents.simple_recommendation_script: Umbrella insurance is like a safety net on top of your auto and home policies. If you're ever sued for more than those policies pay, umbrella coverage steps in. For about $15-30/month per $1M in coverage, it's one of the best values in insurance for protecting your financial future.
insurance_policy_documents.key_exclusions_summary: Liability-only coverage; does not cover your own damages, business activities, or intentional acts.

insurance_policy_documents.simple_description: Replaces a portion of your income if you're temporarily unable to work due to illness or injury. Helps you pay bills while you recover.
ideal_for: Employees without employer-sponsored disability coverage
Self-employed individuals or freelancers
Primary income earners with limited emergency savings
Anyone in a physically demanding job

income_replacement.description: Monthly benefit paid while you're disabled and unable to work
includes: Tax-free benefits if you pay premiums with after-tax dollars
Benefits paid after elimination period (e.g., 14-90 days)

covered_conditions.description: Illnesses and injuries that qualify for benefits
includes: Illnesses: cancer, heart conditions, mental health (if included)

own_occupation_option.description: Higher-tier coverage that pays if you can't do your specific job
includes: Benefits even if you can work in a different role
Ideal for specialized professions (doctors, trades, etc.)
Partial disability benefits for phased return

what_is_not_covered: Pre-existing conditions during initial exclusion period (often 12 months)
Disability from illegal activities, war, or self-inflicted injuries
Normal pregnancy without complications (unless endorsed)
Cosmetic surgery recovery (unless medically necessary)
Unemployment or lack of work (not disability)
Mental/nervous disorders may have limited benefit periods (policy-dependent)
when_to_recommend: User asks: 'What if I get sick and can't work for a few months?'
User asks: 'My employer doesn't offer disability insurance—should I get my own?'
User is self-employed and worried about income loss
User has limited savings and lives paycheck to paycheck
insurance_policy_documents.simple_recommendation_script: Disability insurance protects your most valuable asset: your ability to earn income. If illness or injury keeps you from working, this policy replaces part of your paycheck so you can focus on recovery. We recommend coverage that replaces 60% of income with a 90-day elimination period for best value.
insurance_policy_documents.key_exclusions_summary: Pre-existing conditions excluded initially; mental health benefits may be limited; not for unemployment.

insurance_policy_documents.simple_description: Helps cover veterinary costs for your dog or cat when they get sick or injured. Keep your pet healthy without the financial stress.
ideal_for: Dog or cat owners (most policies)
Owners of breeds prone to health issues
People who want to afford advanced treatments (surgery, cancer care)
Anyone who considers their pet family

includes: Broken bones, cuts, poisoning, foreign object ingestion

illness_coverage.description: Treatment costs for diseases and conditions
includes: Chronic condition management (if enrolled before diagnosis)

flexible_reimbursement.description: Choose how much the policy pays back
includes: Reimbursement levels: 70%, 80%, or 90% of vet bills

what_is_not_covered: Pre-existing conditions (any illness/injury before coverage or during waiting period)
Routine/preventive care (unless wellness rider purchased)
Cosmetic procedures (tail docking, declawing)
Breeding, pregnancy, or birthing costs
Behavioral training or obedience classes
Food, grooming, boarding, or non-medical expenses
Injuries from racing, fighting, or commercial use
when_to_recommend: User asks: 'How can I afford vet bills if my pet gets seriously sick?'
User asks: 'Is pet insurance worth it for a young, healthy dog/cat?'
User has a breed prone to hip dysplasia, heart issues, etc.
User wants to ensure they can say 'yes' to life-saving treatment
insurance_policy_documents.simple_recommendation_script: Pet insurance helps you give your furry friend the best care without worrying about cost. Enroll while your pet is young and healthy to avoid pre-existing condition exclusions. We recommend accident + illness coverage with 80% reimbursement and a $500 deductible for balanced value.
insurance_policy_documents.estimated_premium_range: $25-$70/month for dogs; $15-$45/month for cats (varies by breed, age, location)
insurance_policy_documents.key_exclusions_summary: Pre-existing conditions not covered; routine care requires wellness add-on; waiting periods apply.

rag_optimization_metadata.chunking_strategy: Each policy document is self-contained and can be chunked by: 1) full policy, 2) 'what_is_covered' section, 3) 'what_is_not_covered' section, or 4) 'when_to_recommend' for query matching
embedding_recommendations: Index 'simple_description' and 'simple_recommendation_script' for natural language queries
Use 'ideal_for' and 'when_to_recommend' arrays for intent classification
Tag documents with 'policy_type' for hybrid filtering + semantic search
response_generation_tips: Start with the simple_description to answer in plain language
Reference specific 'what_is_covered' items that match the user's scenario
Proactively mention relevant 'what_is_not_covered' exclusions to set expectations
Use 'simple_recommendation_script' as a template for personalized advice
Include estimated_premium_range when user asks about cost
